The see_also venues don't include pottery-specific museums like the Kyushu Ceramic Museum in Arita.
wrong_data: Karatsu Ware prefecture_codes includes '24' (Mie Prefecture) which appears erroneous — Karatsu ware production is in Saga/Fukuoka area only.
improvement_hint: Apply region_fanout for Kyushu (prefectures 40-47) when query mentions 九州/Kyushu, and filter industry_category to 陶磁器 to focus on pottery. Also add kiln/workshop venue data (not just general museums) to see_also_wikidata_venues for craft queries.

reasoning: The user asked for festivals across the entire Tohoku region (도호쿠 지방 = 6 prefectures: Aomori, Iwate, Miyagi, Akita, Yamagata, Fukushima), but the tool was called only for Aomori prefecture. This is a major coverage failure — only 2 Aomori-specific festivals are returned in the items array. The national_heritage section is padded with many non-Tohoku UNESCO festivals (Saitama, Aichi, Gifu, Mie, Chiba, Toyama, Oita, Tochigi) which are irrelevant to the query. The Korean language requirement (lang=ko) was respected, so RULE D does not apply. Per RULE A, the data returned is real and authoritative but severely incomplete in scope. The non-Tohoku UNESCO entries dilute usefulness. Famous Tohoku festivals like Sendai Tanabata, Akita Kanto, Yamagata Hanagasa, Morioka Sansa Odori are entirely absent.
missing_data: Festivals from Iwate, Miyagi, Akita, Yamagata, Fukushima prefectures; major Tohoku festivals: Sendai Tanabata, Akita Kanto Matsuri, Yamagata Hanagasa Matsuri, Morioka Sansa Odori, Fukushima Waraji Matsuri; region-level fan-out was not applied (region_fanout_applied: false)
wrong_data: national_heritage section contains many non-Tohoku festivals (Saitama, Aichi, Gifu, Mie, Chiba, Toyama, Oita, Tochigi) presented alongside Aomori results, which is misleading for a Tohoku-specific query
improvement_hint: Implement region-level fan-out for Tohoku (prefectures 02-07) when the query toponym is 東北/도호쿠; set region_fanout_applied=true and aggregate results across all 6 prefectures. Filter national_heritage to only include Tohoku-located entries when a regional query is made.
